src/flit/widgets/network_image

      • widgetTypeName(w: NetworkImage): string

Network image widget. Fetches an image over HTTP / HTTPS in a background worker thread, caches the bytes by URL, and renders a placeholder while loading.

Single worker thread per process; fetches are processed FIFO. Cache is per-process and grows unbounded; call clearNetworkImageCache() to release memory.

Currently disabled on JS targets (the browser already has its own async image fetch via <img>; a flit web backend will get a DOM-image variant in a follow-up).

Imports

../foundation/widget, ../foundation/render_object, ../foundation/geometry, ../foundation/color, ../foundation/key, ../foundation/listenable, ../foundation/runtime, ../foundation/binding, basic, image_widget, ../rendering/decoration, ../rendering/text

Types

NetworkFetchStatus = enum
  nfsPending, nfsLoaded, nfsError
NetworkImage = ref object of StatefulWidget
  url*: string
  fit*: ImageFit
  width*, height*: float32
  placeholderColor*: Color
Loads url in a background worker and renders the image once bytes arrive. Shows placeholder while loading or on error; placeholder is built with the placeholder color so the layout slot stays the requested size. 
NetworkImageState = ref object of State

Lets

networkImageTrigger = newValueNotifier(0, nil)
Bumped each time a fetch completes. Currently kept for backwards compatibility; NetworkImage itself no longer subscribes here. Use per-URL notifiers (notifierForUrl) for finer-grained rebuilds.

Procs

proc clearNetworkImageCache() {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Drops every cached fetch result. Pending requests in flight will land in the new cache when they complete. 
proc networkImage(url: string; width: float32 = 0.0; height: float32 = 0.0;
                  fit: ImageFit = ifCover;
                  placeholderColor: Color = rgb(228, 230, 235); key: Key = nil): NetworkImage {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Builds a NetworkImage. Fires an HTTP fetch in a background worker on first build; subsequent builds reuse the cache.

Inputs:

  • url: absolute http(s) URL of the image to load.
  • width, height: layout extents. Both required for stable layout while the image is still loading. If either is 0 the widget collapses to the loaded image's natural size (which means layout jumps once bytes arrive).
  • fit: how to scale the loaded image into the slot. Default ifCover matches typical product-photo cells.
  • placeholderColor: solid color shown while loading or on error. Default light grey.
  • key: optional reconciliation key.

Effect: mounts a StatefulWidget that subscribes to networkImageTrigger so any newly-arrived bytes trigger a rebuild for this widget.

proc networkImageBytes(url: string): string {....raises: [KeyError], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Returns the cached bytes for url, or "" if not yet loaded. 
proc notifierForUrl(url: string): ValueNotifier[int] {....raises: [KeyError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc pumpNetworkImageEvents() {....raises: [KeyError, Exception],
                                tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Called by the runtime once per frame. Drains the list of URLs whose fetches completed since the last call and bumps each URL's own notifier so only the matching widget rebuilds. 
proc requestNetworkImage(url: string): NetworkFetchStatus {.
    ...raises: [ResourceExhaustedError, KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Returns the current cached status for url, kicking off a fetch if not already queued or completed. Safe to call from the main UI thread.

    ...raises: [ResourceExhaustedError, KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
